Emily alleged that she discovered a straw with suspected cocaine residue in her home and insinuated that I was responsible. Despite a lack of any evidence or history of substance abuse on my part, she has repeatedly referenced this fabricated claim to portray me as an unfit parent.

Emily delayed notifying me that LDW had hand, foot, and mouth disease until hours before a scheduled exchange. She refused to provide details about prior medical consultations, leaving me unprepared to care for him appropriately.
Without consulting me, Emily made unilateral decisions regarding LDW’s vaccinations, only informing me afterward.
Emily withheld LDW for over 37 days without a court order, depriving me of parenting time during a critical bonding period, including Easter and Spring Break.
Emily again withheld LDW for 23 days, including Halloween and LDW’s birthday, despite my court-ordered parenting time.
Emily refused a weekly video call schedule, citing discomfort without proposing alternatives.
3. Withholding Parenting Time
Emily withheld LDW for 37 consecutive days without justification, depriving me of meaningful interaction and disrupting our bond.
Emily again withheld LDW, which included Halloween and his birthday, both critical opportunities for bonding and creating positive memories.
Albert Watson served a PPO violation during an exchange, mocking me and creating unnecessary tension. This misuse of legal processes is designed to intimidate and alienate.
Emily’s withholding of Lincoln ends after 25 consecutive days.
Emily continues withholding Lincoln during his birthday week.
Emily leaves for vacation early, taking Lincoln and withholding him through Halloween.
Emily withholds Lincoln during Andrew’s scheduled parenting time.
Emily begins withholding Lincoln again, overlapping Halloween and Andrew’s parenting time.
Emily withholds critical details about Lincoln’s illness, preventing Andrew from adequately preparing for care.
Emily withholds full pediatric records after Andrew requests them per the court order.
Court orders Emily to return Lincoln to Andrew after a month-long withholding.

, during a parenting exchange, LDW referred to Emily’s partner as “Daddy,” despite my consistent presence and active role in his life. Emily refused to address the confusion this causes.

, Emily withheld LDW for 37 consecutive days, ignoring the court-ordered parenting schedule.
, Emily withheld LDW again, including during critical bonding opportunities such as Halloween and his birthday.
, I requested an additional day with LDW to make up for missed time during her vacation. Emily refused, showing no willingness to cooperate.
, LDW’s birthday, Emily refused to allow me any time with him, depriving us of the chance to celebrate together.

, I requested a weekly video chat during off-weeks to maintain LDW’s sense of connection and security. Emily refused without offering an alternative.
, after being withheld for 25 days, LDW demonstrated regression in toilet training, a skill he had previously mastered while in my care.
, LDW referred to Emily’s partner as "Daddy" during an exchange, expressing confusion about his parental roles. This manipulation undermines LDW’s emotional security and erodes his sense of identity.

, LDW’s second birthday, Emily denied my request to see him, depriving us both of an opportunity to celebrate together. Such actions disrupt LDW’s emotional attachment and sense of security in our relationship.
, Emily withheld LDW without providing updates, causing unnecessary confusion and anxiety for him. Upon his return, LDW appeared disoriented and took several days to readjust to his normal routine.

, Emily denied a scheduled video call, citing that LDW “didn’t want to talk.” However, LDW later expressed excitement when he saw me in person, contradicting Emily’s claim and highlighting her alienating behavior.

Defendant engaged in systematic parental alienation: refusal to facilitate contact, disparagement, obstruction of parenting time

Friend of Court failed to investigate or address parental alienation per MCL 552.505
Proper cause and change of circumstances exist under Vodvarka v Grasher to revisit custody: 329+ days separation, alienation, judicial bias
PPO restricts fundamental parental rights without adequate justification — no finding of danger to child
PPO terms are overbroad — restricting all contact including necessary parenting communications
Substantive due process violated: fundamental right to parent arbitrarily restricted without compelling justification
